1	# PRD — spark-modem-watchdog v2
2	
3	| Field         | Value                                          |
4	| ------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
5	| Status        | Draft                                          |
6	| Owner         | TBD (modem platform)                           |
7	| Last updated  | 2026-05-05                                     |
8	| Supersedes    | The v1 toolchain (bash scripts in repo root).  |
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 1. Executive summary
13	
14	`spark-modem-watchdog` is the on-device software that keeps a fleet
15	of Jetson Orin NX bonded-uplink boxes online. It watches four Sierra
16	EM7421 cellular modems, detects when one is unhealthy, and applies
17	the smallest recovery action that has a chance of fixing it — without
18	making things worse. v2 is a from-scratch rewrite that preserves the
19	operational principles proven out in v1 while replacing the
20	implementation, the contracts, and the observability story.
21	
22	## 2. Background
23	
24	### 2.1 What v1 is
25	
26	A pipeline of bash scripts (`diag.sh` → `recovery.sh` → `auto_profile.sh`,
27	`zao_reset_line.sh`) driven by a systemd watchdog. It runs in a 120-second
28	loop: produce JSON diagnostics, decide actions, apply them with backoff
29	and escalation ceilings. Documented in `../README.md`.
30	
31	### 2.2 What v1 got right (we keep these)
32	
33	- **Read-only diag → typed wire format → policy → action tools** as
34	  separate seams.
35	- **Zao log is authoritative** for "is this line bonding?" — never
36	  probe an active line.
37	- **Signal-quality gate**: a reset cannot fix RF, so don't run one
38	  on a modem with insufficient signal.
39	- **Bounded recovery**: escalation ceilings, backoff, one-action-per-
40	  modem-per-cycle, priority order across categories.
41	- **Sysfs-based runtime discovery** of line→cdc-wdm→USB mapping.
42	- **Idempotent operations** at every level.
43	
44	### 2.3 What v1 got wrong (we fix these)
45	
46	- Two-language hybrid (bash + python heredocs) with hand-rolled JSON
47	  encoder; no type checking; fragile parsing of `qmicli` text output.
48	- The contract between diag and recovery is informal: free-form
49	  `detail` strings, heterogeneous `who` fields, no `schema_version`
50	  enforcement.
51	- Recovery counters never decay — modems become permanently
52	  "exhausted" after a single bad incident.
53	- Backoff only blocks the *exact same* action; ping-pong escalation
54	  is possible.
55	- Wall-clock time used for backoff (NTP step can wedge it).
56	- Polling-only architecture; events on the system are ignored.
57	- No tests, no fixtures, no replay harness.
58	- No log rotation, no metrics, no status endpoint, no alerting hook.
59	- Command injection in `auto_profile.sh` (python heredoc with
60	  variable interpolation).
61	- `.bak` files instead of git.
62	
63	The full review is preserved in the project history at
64	`../docs-review-v1.md` (TODO: lift from chat transcript).
65	
66	## 3. Goals (G) and non-goals (NG)
67	
68	### Goals
69	
70	| ID  | Goal                                                           |
71	| --- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
72	| G1  | Maximize end-user uplink availability across the 4 bonded modems. |
73	| G2  | Detect modem health regressions within 30 s of onset.          |
74	| G3  | Apply the minimum-impact recovery action with a >50 % chance of fixing the issue. |
75	| G4  | Never run a destructive recovery that has zero chance of fixing the observed issue (e.g. reset on bad RF). |
76	| G5  | Provision SIM-correct APNs automatically on first boot.        |
77	| G6  | Expose a typed status snapshot and Prometheus-scrape metrics for NOC dashboards. |
78	| G7  | Run the same code under a fake hardware shim for CI tests.     |
79	| G8  | Cleanly survive reboots, SIM swaps, modem flaps, USB hub
80	       resets, and Zao restarts.                                       |
81	| G9  | Be operable by a site technician with shell access and the runbook. |
82	
83	### Non-goals
84	
85	| ID    | Non-goal                                                     |
86	| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
87	| NG1   | Replacing or modifying Zao. We integrate; we do not own it.  |
88	| NG2   | Multi-region carrier support beyond Israel in v2.0. (Carrier table is data; new MCCs land in a config file, not a release.) |
89	| NG3   | Cloud control plane. v2 is a daemon on the box; remote management is out of scope. |
90	| NG4   | Generic "any modem" support. v2 targets Sierra EM7421 + qmi_wwan. Other modems may work but are not tested. |
91	| NG5   | A GUI or web UI on the device. CLI + structured status only. |
92	| NG6   | Replacing `qmicli` as the QMI client. We wrap it.            |
93	
94	## 4. Personas and primary use cases
95	
96	### P1 — Field site technician
97	
98	Installs a fresh-flashed Jetson at a customer site. Has SSH and sudo;
99	no permanent network at install time.
100	
101	- UC1: Run `spark-modem ctl install` and walk away.
102	- UC2: After installation, run `spark-modem ctl status` and see all four lines green.
103	- UC3: When something is red, run `spark-modem ctl diag --explain` and follow the suggested action.
104	
105	### P2 — NOC operator
106	
107	Watches a fleet from a dashboard. Cares about aggregates, alerts.
108	
109	- UC4: See a Prometheus time-series of "modems healthy / total" per box.
110	- UC5: Get a webhook alert when a modem reaches the `Exhausted` state.
111	- UC6: Pull the last 24 h of recovery actions from one box for forensics.
112	
113	### P3 — Field engineer (escalation)
114	
115	Onboards remotely when the site tech and NOC are stuck.
116	
117	- UC7: Tail a structured event log and follow what the watchdog has tried.
118	- UC8: Force a reset state, replay a captured diag JSON in dry-run, run a single action manually.
119	- UC9: Dump the per-modem state machine state and inspect counters.
120	
121	### P4 — Developer (us)
122	
123	Adds a new check, a new recovery action, a new carrier APN.
124	
125	- UC10: Add a new diag check; write a fixture; assert recovery picks the right action.
126	- UC11: Add a new MCC/MNC to the carrier table without a code change.
127	- UC12: Bump the schema version and ensure old/new components fail loudly.
128	
129	## 5. Functional requirements
130	
131	Numbering is stable across revisions. New requirements append at the
132	end; obsolete ones are marked `[withdrawn]`.
133	
134	### 5.1 Discovery and inventory
135	
136	- **FR-1.** The system MUST discover all Sierra-VID modems on the USB
137	  bus at startup and on udev `add`/`remove` events.
138	- **FR-2.** The system MUST resolve each modem to a `(line, cdc_wdm,
139	  usb_path, namespace, iface)` tuple via sysfs, never via hardcoded
140	  paths.
141	- **FR-3.** The system MUST detect SIM identity (ICCID, IMSI) per
142	  modem and persist a `(usb_path → identity)` map across reboots.
143	- **FR-4.** The system MUST detect a SIM swap (ICCID change at the
144	  same `usb_path`) and trigger re-provisioning.
145	
146	### 5.2 Diagnosis
147	
148	- **FR-10.** The system MUST consult Zao's authoritative source
149	  (`zao-remote-endpoint.log` `RASCOW_STAT` lines) before probing a
150	  modem via QMI; if Zao reports the line as `active`, no QMI probe
151	  is run for that line.
152	- **FR-11.** The system MUST gather, per inactive modem: USB speed,
153	  QMI responsiveness, operating mode, SIM card state, SIM application
154	  state, registration state, serving carrier (MCC/MNC/description),
155	  signal (RSSI/RSRP/RSRQ/SNR), profile-1 APN, data session state,
156	  current IPv4.
157	- **FR-12.** The system MUST classify each modem as `Healthy`,
158	  `Degraded(reason)`, `RfBlocked`, `Recovering(level)`, or
159	  `Exhausted` (see [RECOVERY_SPEC.md](RECOVERY_SPEC.md)).
160	- **FR-13.** The system MUST emit a typed `Diag` snapshot every cycle
161	  conforming to [SCHEMA.md § Diag](SCHEMA.md#diag).
162	- **FR-14.** The system MUST detect host-level issues (USB
163	  over-current, "device not accepting address" enumeration failures,
164	  thermal events) from `dmesg` and treat them as global issues.
165	
166	### 5.3 Recovery
167	
168	- **FR-20.** Given a `Diag` snapshot, the system MUST select at most
169	  one recovery action per modem per cycle.
170	- **FR-21.** Action selection MUST follow the priority order
171	  `config > sim > datapath > registration > qmi`. The highest-priority
172	  issue wins; lower-priority issues are deferred to the next cycle.
173	- **FR-22.** Action selection MUST honour the per-modem escalation
174	  ladder defined in [RECOVERY_SPEC.md](RECOVERY_SPEC.md): set_apn,
175	  fix_raw_ip, sim_power_on, soft_reset → modem_reset → usb_reset →
176	  give-up (Exhausted).
177	- **FR-23.** The system MUST gate `modem_reset` and `usb_reset`
178	  actions when the modem's signal is *measurably* below thresholds
179	  (see [RECOVERY_SPEC.md § Gates](RECOVERY_SPEC.md#gates)).
180	- **FR-24.** The system MUST gate the global `driver_reset` to fire
181	  only when ≥75 % of modems are simultaneously QMI-hung AND at least
182	  one of them has actionable signal.
183	- **FR-25.** Same-action backoff MUST suppress repeating any action
184	  on the same modem within `BACKOFF_SECONDS` (default 300 s).
185	- **FR-26.** Per-action escalation counters MUST decay to zero after
186	  `K` consecutive `Healthy` cycles for that modem (see ADR-0006).
187	- **FR-27.** All recovery actions MUST be implemented as separate,
188	  idempotent functions and MUST be runnable individually via the CLI.
189	- **FR-28.** The system MUST provide `--dry-run` everywhere a real
190	  action would mutate state.
191	
192	### 5.4 Provisioning
193	
194	- **FR-30.** The system MUST select the correct APN for a SIM by
195	  looking up `(MCC, MNC)` in a config-file carrier table.
196	- **FR-31.** The system MUST write profile #1 only when the desired
197	  APN differs from the currently programmed value.
198	- **FR-32.** The system MUST verify the post-write APN by reading
199	  the profile back and MUST fail loudly if it does not match.
200	- **FR-33.** New MCC/MNC entries MUST be addable without a code
201	  release (config-file reload).
202	
203	### 5.5 Observability
204	
205	- **FR-40.** The system MUST write a structured event line (JSON
206	  Lines) for every issue observed and every action taken/skipped, to
207	  `/var/log/spark-modem-watchdog/events.jsonl`.
208	- **FR-41.** The system MUST maintain a `status.json` file at
209	  `/var/lib/spark-modem-watchdog/status.json` containing the current
210	  per-modem state, last cycle timestamp, and aggregate health.
211	- **FR-42.** The system MUST expose a Prometheus scrape endpoint on
212	  a configurable Unix socket (default `/run/spark-modem-watchdog/metrics.sock`).
213	- **FR-43.** The system MUST rotate the event log via `logrotate`
214	  with a 7-day, 100 MiB retention default.
215	- **FR-44.** The system MUST emit a webhook POST (configurable URL)
216	  on `Healthy → Degraded` and `Recovering → Exhausted` transitions,
217	  with a typed payload.
218	
219	### 5.6 Operability
220	
221	- **FR-50.** The system MUST ship a single CLI entry point
222	  (`spark-modem`) with subcommands `diag`, `recovery`, `provision`,
223	  `reset`, `status`, `ctl`.
224	- **FR-51.** The CLI MUST accept `--qmi-fixture-dir=PATH` to read
225	  recorded `qmicli` output from disk instead of executing `qmicli`.
226	- **FR-52.** The CLI MUST accept `--diag-fixture=PATH` for the
227	  recovery subcommand to replay a captured snapshot.
228	- **FR-53.** The system MUST run as a systemd unit with `Type=notify`
229	  (so `systemctl status` shows real readiness) and graceful SIGTERM
230	  handling within 5 s.
231	- **FR-54.** Configuration MUST come from, in order of precedence:
232	  command-line flags, environment variables, drop-in YAML files in
233	  `/etc/spark-modem-watchdog/conf.d/`, baked-in defaults.
234	
235	### 5.7 Safety
236	
237	- **FR-60.** The system MUST refuse to start if `qmicli`, `ip`, and
238	  `python3` (≥3.11) are not present on `PATH`.
239	- **FR-61.** The system MUST hold a single PID lock on `/run/spark-modem-watchdog/lock`
240	  for the duration of any state mutation.
241	- **FR-62.** All persistent file writes MUST be atomic (temp file +
242	  rename).
243	- **FR-63.** The system MUST validate every external input
244	  (`qmicli` output, JSON snapshot, Zao log) before acting on it; an
245	  invalid input is a logged error, not a crash.
246	- **FR-64.** The system MUST never `exec` a string built from
247	  external data; all subprocess calls MUST use list-form `argv`.
248	
249	## 6. Non-functional requirements
250	
251	### 6.1 Performance
252	
253	| ID       | Requirement                                                  |
254	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
255	| NFR-1    | A full diag cycle MUST complete in ≤ 10 s on the target Jetson when no modems are unresponsive. |
256	| NFR-2    | The daemon MUST consume ≤ 1 % CPU averaged over a 10-min window in steady state. |
257	| NFR-3    | RSS MUST stay ≤ 80 MiB.                                       |
258	| NFR-4    | Per-modem QMI probes MUST run in parallel (asyncio or threads), not sequentially. |
259	| NFR-5    | Disk write rate MUST stay ≤ 1 MiB/min in steady state (events log + status file). |
260	
261	### 6.2 Reliability
262	
263	| ID       | Requirement                                                  |
264	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
265	| NFR-10   | The daemon MUST recover from any single transient error (parse failure, qmicli timeout, partial fixture) within one cycle. |
266	| NFR-11   | An uncaught exception in the policy engine MUST NOT terminate the daemon; it MUST be logged and the cycle skipped. |
267	| NFR-12   | The daemon MUST tolerate `qmi_wwan` driver reload during operation: a recovery `driver_reset` MUST be observable as a clean state transition, not a daemon crash. |
268	| NFR-13   | The daemon MUST reach steady-state operation within 60 s of process start, given Zao is already running. |
269	
270	### 6.3 Observability
271	
272	| ID       | Requirement                                                  |
273	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
274	| NFR-20   | Every state transition MUST be logged as a single JSON line with fields `ts, modem, from, to, cause, action, dry_run`. |
275	| NFR-21   | The Prometheus exporter MUST expose `actions_total{kind,modem,result}`, `signal_dbm{modem,kind}`, `cycle_duration_seconds`, `modem_state{modem,state}` (gauge with state as label). |
276	| NFR-22   | A snapshot of `status.json` plus the last 200 events MUST be retrievable via a single `spark-modem ctl support-bundle` command for offline analysis. |
277	
278	### 6.4 Security
279	
280	| ID       | Requirement                                                  |
281	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
282	| NFR-30   | Daemon runs as root (it MUST manipulate netns, sysfs, modules), but no other process is granted suid bits. |
283	| NFR-31   | All subprocess calls MUST pass arguments as a list, never a shell string. |
284	| NFR-32   | External text inputs (qmicli output, Zao log, JSON files) MUST be parsed by a validator that rejects unexpected types/shapes. |
285	| NFR-33   | Webhook URLs MUST be validated to be `https://` only by default; `http://` allowed only with an explicit `webhook_allow_http=true` config. |
286	| NFR-34   | No secrets are stored on disk. The webhook signing secret (if used) is read from systemd `LoadCredential=`. |
287	
288	### 6.5 Maintainability
289	
290	| ID       | Requirement                                                  |
291	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
292	| NFR-40   | The codebase MUST pass `mypy --strict`, `ruff`, and a project-wide formatter. |
293	| NFR-41   | Unit tests MUST run without hardware on a developer laptop, using fixtures only. |
294	| NFR-42   | A new MCC/MNC entry MUST be addable in a single YAML edit + reload, without a release. |
295	| NFR-43   | Schema versions are integers (`v1`, `v2`); a daemon MUST refuse to load a snapshot/state file from a future schema and MUST migrate or refuse a snapshot from a known-old schema with an explicit message. |
296	
297	## 7. Constraints
298	
299	### 7.1 Hardware
300	
301	- **C1.** NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX (16 GB) on P3768 reference carrier.
302	- **C2.** 4× Sierra Wireless EM7421 (VID:PID `1199:9091`) on a USB 3
303	  hub plugged into one carrier USB-A port, typically enumerating on
304	  `3610000.xhci/usb2/2-3/2-3.1/2-3.1.{1..4}`.
305	
306	### 7.2 Software
307	
308	- **C10.** JetPack 5.1.5 / L4T R35.6.4 / Ubuntu 20.04 / aarch64.
309	- **C11.** Kernel 5.10-tegra with `qmi_wwan` and `cdc_wdm` modules.
310	- **C12.** Soliton Zao SDK 2.1.0+ (`ZaoInfraCtrl` + `ZaoRemoteEndpointCloud`).
311	- **C13.** ModemManager MUST remain disabled (Zao requires exclusive
312	  modem access).
313	- **C14.** Python ≥ 3.11 available system-wide (it ships with Ubuntu
314	  20.04 via `python3.11` PPA, or we bundle it in a venv).
315	
316	### 7.3 Network
317	
318	- **C20.** Install-time the box MAY be offline; the installer MUST
319	  not require internet.
320	- **C21.** No outbound dependencies at runtime except the optional
321	  alert webhook.
322	
323	### 7.4 Regulatory
324	
325	- **C30.** No code change is required for new MCC/MNC entries; they
326	  are configuration data.
327	
328	## 8. Success metrics
329	
330	These are measured on the production fleet 90 days after v2.0 ships
331	across the fleet. v2 is considered successful if it meets all of:
332	
333	- **M1.** ≥ 99.5 % per-modem availability over a rolling 7 days, where
334	  availability = `(seconds_in_Healthy + seconds_in_Recovering) / total_seconds`.
335	- **M2.** Median MTTR (from `Healthy → Degraded` to `Degraded → Healthy`)
336	  ≤ 60 s for SIM-app issues, ≤ 90 s for registration issues, ≤ 180 s
337	  for QMI-hung issues.
338	- **M3.** False-positive destructive resets (a `modem_reset` or
339	  `usb_reset` that did not change signal/registration outcome within
340	  one cycle) ≤ 5 % of all destructive resets fleet-wide.
341	- **M4.** Zero `Exhausted` states caused by counter accumulation
342	  rather than a real recurring fault. Verified by running the
343	  decay-on-healthy logic against 30 days of replay traces.
344	- **M5.** P99 cycle duration ≤ 10 s.
345	- **M6.** Zero out-of-memory or unhandled-exception daemon restarts
346	  in any 30-day window across the fleet.
347	- **M7.** Dev-laptop test suite runs in ≤ 30 s on a typical laptop.
348	
349	## 9. Out of scope (v2.0)
350	
351	- Carrier table for non-Israeli MCCs (data, not code; can be added
352	  post-launch via config).
353	- Multi-SIM (eSIM) management. EM7421 is single-SIM.
354	- 5G NR-only operation (the modem is LTE+NR; we currently treat NR
355	  as "informational" — full NR-aware policy is v2.1).
356	- Cellular-only fleet management (assumes Zao bonding is the uplink).
357	- Hot-plug of modems mid-flight (we support it via udev events but
358	  do not prioritise it; SLA on FR-1 covers boot-time discovery).
359	- Cross-vendor modems (Quectel, Telit, etc.).

1	# spark-modem-watchdog v2
2	
3	## What This Is
4	
5	`spark-modem-watchdog` is the on-device daemon that keeps a fleet of NVIDIA
6	Jetson Orin NX bonded-uplink boxes online by watching four Sierra Wireless
7	EM7421 cellular modems, detecting when one is unhealthy, and applying the
8	smallest recovery action that has a chance of fixing it without making things
9	worse. v2 is a from-scratch Python rewrite that preserves the operational
10	principles proven out in the v1 bash toolchain while replacing the
11	implementation, the wire contracts, and the observability story.
12	
13	## Core Value
14	
15	Maximize end-user uplink availability across the four bonded modems by
16	applying minimum-impact recovery actions — and never running a destructive
17	recovery (modem/USB reset) that has zero chance of fixing the observed
18	issue (e.g. reset on bad RF).
19

150	## Context
151	
152	**Production reality.** v1 is a pipeline of bash scripts (`diag.sh` →
153	`recovery.sh` → `auto_profile.sh`, `zao_reset_line.sh`) driven by a systemd
154	watchdog on a 120 s loop. It currently keeps a real fleet online; v2 must
155	prove itself in shadow mode before replacing v1 on any box (see Migration
156	phases 1-2).
157	
158	**What v1 got wrong (motivates the rewrite).**
159	- Two-language hybrid (bash + python heredocs); hand-rolled JSON; fragile
160	  `awk -F"'"` parsing of `qmicli`.
161	- Free-form `detail` strings and heterogeneous `who` field — no type checking
162	  on the wire (replaced by typed enums + tagged union, ADR-0004).
163	- Recovery counters never decay → modems become permanently Exhausted after
164	  one bad incident (fixed by counter-decay-on-healthy, ADR-0006).
165	- Wall-clock backoff (NTP step can wedge it) → all backoff math is on
166	  `time.monotonic()` in v2 (ADR-0007).
167	- Polling-only architecture; events ignored → v2 subscribes to udev,
168	  rtnetlink, inotify, dmesg with polling fallback (ADR-0002).
169	- Command injection in `auto_profile.sh` via shell-string interpolation;
170	  `.bak` files instead of git; no tests, no fixtures, no replay harness;
171	  no log rotation; no metrics; no status endpoint.
172	
173	**Domain glue.** Zao (Soliton's bonding stack) owns the modems at runtime;
174	the watchdog observes around it. Zao's `RASCOW_STAT` log line tells us which
175	of lines 1..4 are currently bonding — never QMI-probe a Zao-active line
176	(ADR-0003). Zao's `InfraCtrl.script` owns profile programming; we invoke it
177	rather than writing profiles directly so Zao observes the change.
178	
179	**Carrier table.** Israeli MCC 425 with MNC entries for Partner (01),
180	Cellcom (02), Pelephone (03). New MCC/MNC entries are pure config (YAML
181	edit + reload) — never a code release.
182	
183	**Observability targets.** NOC consumes Prometheus metrics + webhook alerts.
184	Field engineers consume `events.jsonl` (replay-able) and the support bundle
185	(`spark-modem ctl support-bundle` produces a tarball with the last 200
186	events, current `status.json`, all per-modem state, journal slice, dmesg
187	slice).
188	
189	## Constraints
190	
191	- **Hardware**: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX (16 GB) on P3768 reference carrier — C1
192	- **Hardware**: 4× Sierra Wireless EM7421 (VID:PID `1199:9091`) on USB 3 hub, typically `2-3.1.{1..4}` — C2
193	- **Software**: JetPack 5.1.5 / L4T R35.6.4 / Ubuntu 20.04 / aarch64 / kernel 5.10-tegra — C10/C11
194	- **Software**: Soliton Zao SDK 2.1.0+; `ModemManager` MUST remain disabled (Zao requires exclusive modem access) — C12/C13
195	- **Language (target runtime)**: **Python 3.8.10** is the actual Jetson system Python and the runtime we must support on-box. ADR-0001 / docs/ assumed 3.11+; that conflict is open and must be resolved during the research/planning phases (options: bundle 3.11 in a venv as ADR-0001 originally proposed, drop to 3.8-compatible code, or upgrade the Jetson runtime). The pydantic v2, `match` statements, asyncio TaskGroup, `tomllib`, and various typing features the docs use are 3.10/3.11 — careful inventory needed.
196	- **Schema/types**: pydantic v2 for all wire formats; closed enums for `IssueCategory` / `IssueDetail` / `RegistrationState` etc. — ADR-0004
197	- **Network**: Install-time the box MAY be offline; installer MUST not require internet — C20
198	- **Network**: Zero outbound runtime dependencies except optional alert webhook — C21
199	- **Performance**: P99 cycle ≤ 10 s; RSS ≤ 80 MiB; 1 % CPU steady-state — NFR-1/3/2
200	- **Process**: Single-process `asyncio` daemon; per-modem probes via `asyncio.gather` with per-task timeout (default 8 s); single `asyncio.Lock` guards state-store commits — ARCH § 4.3
201	- **Time**: All durations and backoffs use `time.monotonic()`; `time.time()` only for ISO-8601 stamps — ADR-0007
202	- **Subprocess**: Every external command via one wrapper, list-form argv, with timeout — FR-64 / NFR-31
203	
204	## Key Decisions
205	
206	| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
207	|----------|-----------|---------|
208	| Python 3.11+, single-process asyncio daemon, `.deb` with bundled venv | ADR-0001: typed wire formats need real types; `qmicli` text parsing is awkward in any language; team velocity is best in Python | ⚠️ Revisit — Jetson system Python is 3.8.10; resolve via venv-bundled 3.11 (ADR-0001's original plan), 3.8-compatible code, or runtime upgrade |
209	| Event-driven core (udev / rtnetlink / inotify / dmesg) with 30 s polling fallback (down from v1's 120 s) | ADR-0002: cycle is much cheaper now; events shorten median MTTR | — Pending |
210	| Zao `RASCOW_STAT` is authoritative for "is line N bonding"; never QMI-probe a Zao-active line | ADR-0003: avoids QMI control-channel race; Zao's view is correct by definition | — Pending |
211	| Strict typed JSON contract (pydantic v2, closed enums, `schema_version` int, tagged-union `who`) | ADR-0004: v1's free-form `detail` strings + heterogeneous `who` caused silent regressions | — Pending |
212	| Explicit per-modem state machine: `unknown` / `healthy` / `degraded` / `recovering(level)` / `rf_blocked` / `exhausted` / `disconnected` | ADR-0005: makes recovery decisions auditable; spec-as-tests against the markdown table | — Pending |
213	| Per-action escalation counters decay to zero after K consecutive Healthy cycles (default K=10) | ADR-0006: fixes v1's permanent-`Exhausted` failure mode after a single bad incident | — Pending |
214	| All backoff arithmetic uses `time.monotonic()`; wall clock only for ISO-8601 stamps | ADR-0007: NTP step on the Jetson can wedge wall-clock backoff | — Pending |
215	| Policy engine is a pure function `Diag × {ModemState, Globals, Config, Clock} → PlannedAction[]` — no subprocess, no I/O | Testability: every decision-table row in RECOVERY_SPEC § 4 is a fixture | — Pending |
216	| Six-phase migration (bench → field box dry-run → field box live → 10 % canary → 100 %) before v1 decommission | v1 keeps a real fleet online; conservative cutover; rollback button at every phase | — Pending |
217	| CLI-only ctl tool (no HTTP API on Unix socket) for v2.0 | Open question Q1 deferred to v2.1; minimize surface area | — Pending |

1	# ADR-0014 — v1-Retired Scope Pivot
2	
3	| Field        | Value                  |
4	| ------------ | ---------------------- |
5	| Status       | Accepted               |
6	| Date         | 2026-05-11             |
7	| Deciders     | Ops + Eng team         |
8	| Supersedes   | MIGRATION.md Phases 1-2 (shadow-alongside-v1 strategy) |
9	
10	## Context
11	
12	The original migration plan (docs/MIGRATION.md, written 2026-05-05) assumed
13	v1 and v2 would run side-by-side during a multi-phase shadow period:
14	
15	- Phase 1: v2 deployed as `spark-modem-watchdog-v2.service` alongside v1.
16	- Phase 2: v2 active in shadow mode, v1 remains primary.
17	- Phase 3: cutover — stop v1, move v2 to canonical paths.
18	
19	This plan required shadow-mode artifacts (`99-shadow.yaml`,
20	`tools/compare_v1_v2.py`, `-v2`-suffixed systemd unit and paths) and assumed
21	a rollback path via `spark-modem-watchdog-v1_1.0.0_all.deb`.
22	
23	By 2026-05-11, v1 had already been retired across the entire fleet. The bash
24	scripts (`diag.sh`, `recovery.sh`, `auto_profile.sh`, `zao_reset_line.sh`)
25	are no longer running on any box. v1 was never packaged as a `.deb`; it was
26	deployed as loose scripts. Building a v1 `.deb` now would mean packaging
27	unmaintained code that has not run in production for days, creating false
28	confidence in a rollback path that was never tested.
29	
30	## Decision
31	
32	**v2 is the only daemon. v1 will not be rebuilt or repackaged.**
33	
34	Specifically:
35	
36	1. The shadow-alongside-v1 migration strategy (MIGRATION.md Phases 1-2) is
37	   retired. v2 deploys directly to canonical paths:
38	   - Service: `spark-modem-watchdog.service` (no `-v2` suffix).
39	   - Install root: `/opt/spark-modem-watchdog/`.
40	   - State directory: `/var/lib/spark-modem-watchdog/`.
41	
42	2. Rollback means v2 previous version only (`apt install
43	   spark-modem-watchdog=<prev-version>`). The apt repository retains the
44	   last 3 `.deb` releases to support this.
45	
46	3. No v1 `.deb` will be built. The v1 bash scripts are archived in the
47	   `v1-legacy` branch for reference only.
48	
49	4. All `-v2`-suffixed paths, `99-shadow.yaml`, and `tools/compare_v1_v2.py`
50	   are removed from documentation and will not be built.
51	
52	## Consequences
53	
54	- **MIGRATION.md rewrite required.** Phases 1-2 (shadow mode) are dead.
55	  Phase 3 simplifies to: `apt install` + `systemctl enable --now` + verify
56	  healthy within 60 s.
57	- **Rollback story is honest.** Operators know the rollback target is the
58	  previous v2 `.deb`, not a v1 that was never packaged. This removes a
59	  false safety net and forces proper canary gating.
60	- **No shadow-mode code or tooling.** `compare_v1_v2.py` is never built.
61	  `99-shadow.yaml` is never written. The `-v2`-suffixed systemd unit is
62	  never created. This eliminates ~200 lines of dead migration tooling from
63	  the project scope.
64	- **Fleet cutover simplifies.** Per-box procedure becomes a single `apt
65	  install` + service enable. Health-gate validation replaces the
66	  side-by-side comparison as the primary safety mechanism.
67	- **Risk accepted:** if v2 has a catastrophic bug on a box, the only
68	  recovery is downgrade to previous v2 version or manual intervention.
69	  There is no automated path back to bash scripts. This is mitigated by
70	  the 3-phase canary rollout (site, region, fleet) with health-gate
71	  holds between phases.
72	
73	## Revisit when
74	
75	- A customer or compliance requirement demands a tested rollback to a
76	  fundamentally different daemon implementation (not just a version
77	  downgrade). This would require building and qualifying a separate
78	  rollback package, which is not justified today.
79
